questions.question: What is the period of the function y = sin(2x)?
questions.explanation: The period of y = sin(bx) is 2π/b. Here b = 2, so period = 2π/2 = π.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 50°, angle B = 70°, and side a = 10. Find the length of side b using the Law of Sines.
questions.explanation: Law of Sines: a/sin(A) = b/sin(B). So 10/sin(50°) = b/sin(70°). Thus b = 10·sin(70°)/sin(50°) ≈ 11.2.

questions.question: Where does the graph of y = cos(x) intersect the x-axis in the interval [0, 2π]?
questions.explanation: cos(x) = 0 when x = π/2 + nπ for integer n. In [0, 2π], these are π/2 and 3π/2.

questions.question: A triangle has sides a = 5, b = 6, and c = 7. Find cos(C) using the Law of Cosines.
questions.explanation: Law of Cosines: c² = a² + b² − 2ab·cos(C). So 49 = 25 + 36 − 60·cos(C), giving 60·cos(C) = 12, thus cos(C) = 1/5. Wait: 49 = 61 − 60·cos(C), so cos(C) = 12/60 = 1/5.

questions.question: What is the amplitude of y = 3sin(x) − 2?
questions.explanation: The amplitude is the coefficient of the sine function, which is |3| = 3. The −2 is a vertical shift, not part of amplitude.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, side p = 8, side q = 9, and angle Q = 60°. Find angle P using the Law of Sines.
